conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
So you're saying your commute was normal this week?
Yeah, just the usual morning routine. I didn't really think about much until I got to work, though.
What was going on at work that was on your mind?
Just trying to meet some deadlines. I've been working on a big project and it's weighing on me a bit.
Can you tell me more about this project? How's it making you feel?
It's just feeling really overwhelming, you know? Like I'm not good enough or something.
That sounds stressful. Have you noticed any changes in your daily routine that might be contributing to these feelings?
Well, I did have to cancel plans with some friends last weekend because of work. Maybe that didn't help.
It sounds like you've been prioritizing work over social time. How do you feel about that?
Honestly, I just feel guilty for not being there for my friends when they needed me.
You're feeling a sense of guilt and regret about not being available to your friends?
Yeah, exactly. Like I should be there for them more often.
That can be really tough to navigate. Have you talked to anyone else about how you're feeling? Maybe someone at work or in your social circle?
Actually, my best friend and I were talking the other day, and she said I'm being too hard on myself sometimes.
It sounds like she's giving you some tough love! How did that make you feel?
Honestly, it kind of helped. I mean, I know she's right, but it's still hard to shake off this feeling of inadequacy.
That makes sense. You're recognizing your friend's perspective, but the feelings are still there. Can you tell me more about what "inadequate" means to you?
I guess just that I'm not doing enough or being good enough in some way.
It sounds like this feeling of inadequacy is tied up with your guilt and regret around work and social commitments. How do you think that's impacting your relationships with others?
Honestly, I think it's causing me to push people away a bit. Like, I don't want to let anyone down or disappoint them.
That makes sense given how much pressure you're putting on yourself. Are there any small things you've been enjoying lately that help take your mind off of work?
Actually, yeah! I've been trying out this new recipe for chicken parmesan and it's really hit the spot.
Mmm, that sounds delicious! It's great that you're finding some joy in cooking. Can we explore how you balance taking care of yourself with your busy work schedule?
Yeah, definitely. I mean, sometimes I'll just order takeout or grab something quick if I don't have time to cook.
That's a good coping mechanism! Do you feel like that helps alleviate some of the guilt and pressure around not being able to do everything perfectly?
Yeah, kind of. It's just hard to remember that sometimes.
Absolutely. Remembering to prioritize self-care is an ongoing process. What are your goals for our work together in this session? Is there anything specific you'd like to focus on or change?
Honestly, I guess I just want to figure out how to balance my guilt and regret with being kind to myself.

That's a great starting point! We can definitely explore strategies for addressing that. One thing that might be helpful is setting small, achievable goals for yourself outside of work or social commitments. What do you think about trying something like that?
Yeah, I could try that. Just not sure what specifically.
That's okay! We can brainstorm some ideas together. What's something you've been wanting to try or explore that's not related to work or social obligations?
Hmm... actually, I've always wanted to learn how to play the guitar.
That sounds like a great hobby to explore! Why don't we set a goal for you to look into local music classes or online tutorials and see if you can find something that interests you?
Sounds doable. Yeah, that would be really cool.

Awesome! I'm glad we could start exploring some of these ideas together. One thing before we wrap up - is there anything in particular you're looking forward to or hoping will change after our work together?
Honestly, just being able to let go of some of this guilt and regret. Being kinder to myself.

That's a beautiful goal! I think we've made some great progress today. Remember that taking small steps towards self-care and prioritizing your own well-being is a process, and it's okay to take things one step at a time. We'll work on building on these insights in our next session.
Sounds good. Thanks for listening.
You're welcome. See you soon!
